jokes.text: DISCO SUCKS!
jokes.setup: Disco music playing, establishing the disco setting
jokes.punchline: Ken's immediate, aggressive rejection of disco
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ken's anti-disco stance drives his arc. Simple, economical, quotable.

jokes.text: I'm only gonna let girls sign it, 'cause that way, when I have kids, they'll think I was a big stud.
jokes.setup: Sam talking about the yearbook
jokes.punchline: His plan to fabricate a studly past for his future children
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Sam's insecurity and desire to appear cool drives this delusional but endearing plan.

jokes.text: Never a wild and sexy guy.
jokes.setup: Girls always write 'Neal, you're a wild and crazy guy'
jokes.punchline: Neal's disappointment at never being called 'wild and sexy'
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Neal's specific desires and disappointments with his image.

jokes.text: Where do they find time to play sports?
jokes.setup: Neal listing all the pranks jocks do
jokes.punchline: His logical question about their time management
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation - Neal's analytical mind applied to jock behavior creates genuine insight and humor.

jokes.text: What's so geeky about us, anyways? We're just guys.
jokes.setup: Sam questioning why they're called geeks
jokes.punchline: Harris immediately enters with D&D handbook, perfectly answering the question
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy structure - Sam's denial is immediately contradicted by Harris's D&D entrance. Great dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Oh, no! I'd never do that.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if she'd tell about the cheating
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's obviously sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcastic response - functional but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: These things are gonna kill me. I know I might look cool with a cigarette in my hand, but, remember, it's just a crutch.
jokes.setup: Teacher smoking while talking to students
jokes.punchline: His self-aware anti-smoking message while actively smoking
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy - the teacher's self-aware contradiction feels authentic and absurd.

jokes.text: This is a graph of their lives. [demonstrates with hand gestures]
jokes.setup: Mr. Fleck explaining jock life trajectory
jokes.punchline: His dramatic hand gestures mapping the rise and fall
jokes.explanation: Clever visual comedy - using hand gestures to graph a life trajectory is both absurd and insightful.

jokes.text: That's the pinnacle of their lives.
jokes.setup: Describing jocks cleaning out the geeks
jokes.punchline: This moment being their life's peak
jokes.explanation: Dark but insightful observation - the cruel truth delivered with perfect timing and economy.

jokes.text: And yes, Mr. Jock-who-cleaned-me-out, I will have fries with that.
jokes.setup: Building up the geeks' future success
jokes.punchline: The role reversal with the jock serving them
jokes.explanation: Satisfying revenge fantasy punchline - the 'fries with that' cliché works well in this context.

jokes.text: But that's like a long time from now. We have to put up with this for another 3 years.
jokes.setup: Mr. Fleck's inspiring speech about future success
jokes.punchline: Neal's practical concern about the immediate reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the motivational moment - Neal's practical mind cuts through the fantasy.

jokes.text: A 16-millimeter print of Monty Python and the Holy Grail?
jokes.setup: Mr. Fleck talking about simple pleasures
jokes.punchline: Harris's ecstatic reaction to the film print
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Harris's A.V. geek enthusiasm for rare film prints feels authentic.

jokes.text: I'm so happy, I think I'm gonna cry.
jokes.setup: Harris explaining the film trade
jokes.punchline: His overwhelming emotional response to A.V. paradise
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of Harris's A.V. geek persona - his extreme emotional investment is endearing.

jokes.text: It was hilarious.
jokes.setup: Dave Fleury broke his arm falling off gym ropes
jokes.punchline: Student's casual enjoyment of another's injury
jokes.explanation: Dark teen humor - the casual cruelty of finding injury 'hilarious' feels authentic to high school.

jokes.text: You don't think you're the Fonz or something? If a jukebox was broken, think you could hit it and it'd start playing?
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso confronting Daniel about being cool
jokes.punchline: The specific Fonzie jukebox reference
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific reference - Mr. Rosso's Fonzie comparison captures both the era and Daniel's image.

jokes.text: Do you like them apples?
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso trying to be tough with Daniel
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at intimidating slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Rosso character moment - his attempt to be tough comes across as awkward and endearing.

jokes.text: DO YOU LIKE THEM APPLES?
jokes.setup: Rosso trying to sound tough while punishing Daniel
jokes.punchline: Using a corny catchphrase to sound intimidating
jokes.explanation: Rosso trying to be tough with dated slang perfectly captures his awkward authority.

jokes.text: You're quitting?
jokes.setup: Ms. Kelly says the announcement will make someone very happy
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's hopeful guess that she's quitting
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline structure - Lindsay's hopeful misinterpretation of the setup creates perfect comedy.

jokes.text: I'm one lucky girl?
jokes.setup: Ms. Kelly expecting Lindsay to be thrilled about the academic summit
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's unenthusiastic, questioning repetition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - her reluctant compliance with what she's 'supposed' to feel creates authentic teen awkwardness.

jokes.text: Are the kids in Michigan schools that stupid?
jokes.setup: Lindsay questioning how she's in the top 1%
jokes.punchline: Her logical conclusion about other students
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation - Lindsay's logical but insulting conclusion about Michigan students is both funny and character-revealing.

jokes.text: When I was in college, back in the early 1700s
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso talking about his college days
jokes.punchline: His self-deprecating exaggeration of his age
jokes.explanation: Nice self-deprecating humor from Mr. Rosso - the extreme exaggeration feels authentic to his character.

jokes.text: I heard that's because you could see a girl's boobs in it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the 'time of the month' film
jokes.punchline: Sam's matter-of-fact explanation about the real reason for exclusion
jokes.explanation: Authentic teen boy humor - Sam's focus on the sexual aspect rather than the educational purpose feels realistic.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. He's putting him in A.V. as punishment?
jokes.setup: Learning Daniel is being placed in A.V.
jokes.punchline: Harris's realization that his beloved A.V. is seen as punishment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Harris's hurt realization that his paradise is others' punishment creates sympathy and humor.

jokes.text: I bet that Daniel guy is high on drugs, and if we make him angry, he'll freak out and wreck the A.V. room.
jokes.setup: The geeks worried about Daniel joining A.V.
jokes.punchline: Bill's dramatic, paranoid scenario
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Bill's paranoid imagination and fear of the 'dangerous' Daniel feels authentic to geek anxiety.

jokes.text: Sam, he gave you a porno. I wouldn't exactly say you have a meaningful relationship with him.
jokes.setup: Sam claiming to know Daniel
jokes.punchline: Neal's logical assessment of their 'relationship'
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback and logical observation - Neal's practical assessment of Sam's 'friendship' with Daniel is both funny and accurate.

jokes.text: Look what it did for me!
jokes.setup: Mr. Fleck mentioning he was in the 1% club in 1956
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic assessment of how well it worked out for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-deprecating humor - Mr. Fleck's awareness of his current situation versus his past promise creates great irony.

jokes.text: I wish I'd never heard it, just so I could hear it again for the first time.
jokes.setup: Girl talking about American Beauty being the best album
jokes.punchline: Her paradoxical wish to unhear it to experience it fresh
jokes.explanation: Beautifully crafted line - captures the Deadhead mentality and the universal desire to re-experience great art with fresh eyes.

jokes.text: When does Alan Funt come boogieing out?
jokes.setup: Ken watching Nick's disco transformation
jokes.punchline: His Candid Camera reference suggesting this must be a prank
jokes.explanation: Great period reference - Ken's disbelief at Nick's transformation expressed through perfect 1980 cultural reference.

jokes.text: What's the difference between Zeppelin and disco, really? They both have heavy drums and they have bass.
jokes.setup: Nick trying to justify his disco conversion
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous oversimplification of musical genres
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick logic - his simplistic musical analysis to justify his disco phase shows his rationalization process.

jokes.text: No! I don't know that song, thank God. But I can guarantee it sucks.
jokes.setup: Nick asking if Ken knows the Foxy song
jokes.punchline: Ken's emphatic dismissal without even hearing it
jokes.explanation: Good Ken character consistency - his anti-disco stance is so strong he'll condemn music he's never heard.

jokes.text: Lindsay, can you please start going out with Nick again? I can't take much more of this.
jokes.setup: Ken dealing with Nick's disco phase
jokes.punchline: His desperate plea to Lindsay to fix the situation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Ken's desperation reaches the point where he's willing to sacrifice his friend's happiness for musical integrity.

jokes.text: If I was wearing my vest, all the buttons would pop.
jokes.setup: Harold expressing pride about Lindsay's achievement
jokes.punchline: His folksy saying about bursting with pride
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold character moment - his old-fashioned expression of pride feels authentic and endearing.

jokes.text: [FAKE COUGHS] Geek!
jokes.setup: Daniel operating the projector in class
jokes.punchline: Student cough-calling Daniel a geek
jokes.explanation: Basic schoolyard humor - the fake cough insult is predictable but serves to show Daniel's social fall from grace.

jokes.text: Will somebody please help Mr. DeMille set up the projector?
jokes.setup: Daniel struggling with the projector
jokes.punchline: Teacher comparing him to the famous director Cecil B. DeMille
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural reference - the DeMille comparison adds some wit to the teacher's frustration with Daniel's incompetence.

jokes.text: SUDDENLY EVERYBODY GOES RUNNING DOWN TO THE PIT, AND THEY START DANCING IN THE MUD. AND THEN THE SUN COMES OUT, AND THERE'S THIS RAINBOW RIGHT OVER THE STAGE. I STARTED CRYING.
jokes.setup: Girl describing Grateful Dead concert experience
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top hippie spiritual experience description
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of hippie concert stories with all the expected elements.

jokes.text: Why don't you go tell it to the fire alarm?
jokes.setup: Daniel complaining about his problems
jokes.punchline: Kim's reference to his fire alarm pulling
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback - Kim's sarcastic reference to Daniel's earlier action shows her frustration and wit.

jokes.text: You always fall down a well trying to steal stuff.
jokes.setup: Bill wanting to be a thief again
jokes.punchline: Sam's reference to Bill's consistent D&D failures
jokes.explanation: Nice character continuity - shows the history of their gaming sessions and Bill's consistent bad luck.

jokes.text: I've had a crush on you since the sixth grade. I always sat behind you, even though you were so tall I couldn't see the board.
jokes.setup: Nick explaining his feelings for Sara
jokes.punchline: His admission of academic self-sabotage for love
jokes.explanation: Sweet and funny character moment - Nick's romantic sacrifice of his education for proximity to his crush is both endearing and absurd.

jokes.text: What, you just send your sword into battle for you, and then you stay home?
jokes.setup: Learning about the dancing sword in D&D
jokes.punchline: Daniel's logical but literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Great outsider perspective - Daniel's practical assessment of D&D mechanics highlights the absurdity from a non-geek viewpoint.

jokes.text: You get to pretend to be somebody you can't be in real life.
jokes.setup: Sam explaining D&D to Daniel
jokes.punchline: His honest assessment of the game's appeal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight - Sam's explanation captures the essence of escapist gaming and teen insecurity in one perfect line.

jokes.text: You can't possibly be any worse than Bill.
jokes.setup: Daniel worried about being bad at D&D
jokes.punchline: Neal's reassurance that throws Bill under the bus
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with character consistency - Neal's backhanded encouragement at Bill's expense feels authentic.

jokes.text: Even the Stones know it. They're willing to admit it. Why can't you?
jokes.setup: Disco guy defending disco
jokes.punchline: Using the Rolling Stones' Miss You as evidence
jokes.explanation: Smart musical argument - using the Stones' disco experiment as evidence is both historically accurate and rhetorically clever.

jokes.text: Carlos the Dwarf?
jokes.setup: Daniel naming his D&D character Carlos
jokes.punchline: Sam's incredulous reaction to the incongruous name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel moment - his choice of a very un-fantasy name for his fantasy character shows his outsider status and practical mindset.

jokes.text: A DWARF? I DON'T WANNA BE A DWARF. I WANTED TO BE, LIKE, A NINJA OR A FIGHTER.
jokes.setup: Daniel rolling dice to determine his D&D character
jokes.punchline: His disappointment at getting an unglamorous character type
jokes.explanation: Daniel's disappointment at getting a less cool character shows his investment in being impressive.

jokes.text: Yeah, you got a problem with that, Gorthon?
jokes.setup: Sam questioning the name Carlos
jokes.punchline: Daniel's defensive response using Bill's ridiculous D&D name
jokes.explanation: Great comeback structure - Daniel using Bill's silly name to defend his own choice shows quick wit and confidence.

jokes.text: AND YOU'RE TOO TALL TO BE A GOOD DANCER!
jokes.setup: Ken's final insult after the disco confrontation
jokes.punchline: His desperate reach for any insult about Nick
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ken's desperation to insult Nick, grasping at straws.

jokes.text: Does him wanting to play with us again mean that he's turning into a geek... or we're turning into cool guys?
jokes.setup: Daniel enjoying D&D and wanting to play again
jokes.punchline: Bill's philosophical question about the social implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical observation - Bill's question about social status and identity gets to the heart of the show's themes. Perfectly crafted and deeply quotable.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go for us being cool guys.
jokes.setup: Bill's question about their social status
jokes.punchline: Sam's optimistic interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sam response - his hopeful interpretation shows his desire for social acceptance while maintaining his loyalty to his friends.

jokes.text: That, and we pin her name and address on her jacket in case she gets lost.
jokes.setup: Neal explaining they give grandma chocolates when putting her on the bus
jokes.punchline: The additional detail about treating her like a lost child
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Neal's additional detail about treating his grandmother like a lost child adds dark humor to the sweet gesture.

jokes.text: It didn't cost me a cent.
jokes.setup: Bill giving Lindsay a gift
jokes.punchline: His honest admission that the gift was free
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - his guileless honesty about the free gift shows his lack of social filters and sweet intentions.
